item.description: Prime Minister of Pakistan Shehbaz Sharif announced that a deal between the U.S. and Iran has been reached with a signing expected on Friday in Switzerland. NBC News' Keir Simmons, Gabe Gutierrez and Matt Bradley report.

media:content.media:title: Deal between U.S., Iran has been reached says Pakistan's prime minister
media:content.media:description: Prime Minister of Pakistan Shehbaz Sharif announced that a deal between the U.S. and Iran has been reached with a signing expected on Friday in Switzerland. NBC News' Keir Simmons, Gabe Gutierrez and Matt Bradley report.

item.title: Deal between U.S., Iran has been reached says Pakistan's prime minister

item.dc:creator: Gary Grumbach
Natalie Keyssar
Owen Hayes
item.description: Ahead of the UFC fight card on the White House lawn, thousands descended on Washington D.C. to celebrate the sport, country and President Trump.

media:content.media:text: Thousands of fans gathered Saturday afternoon on the Ellipse for Freedom 250’s “Fan Fest” event, a sprawling fair of booths and vendors set up in the park south of the White House to celebrate the UFC Fight planned for Sunday night.
Washington, D.C., National Guard members pose for a photos with the "Monster Energy girls."
A split composite image of Diane Scalice standing at a park, and a close up of her Donald Trump socks with fuzzy blond threads sticking out for his hair
Sponsors like Crypto.com, Meta, Monster Energy and Ram Trucks joined UFC-themed games and photo-ops for which fans waited in line for hours to participate.

“I want to get a chance to see (Sean) O'Malley breathe the same air,” said Desiree Jones, of Maryland. Akawsi Mensah, also of Maryland, said: “This is like life-changing. America turns 250, UFC in DC. It's like I can't miss this for the world.”

Christopher Shane Tanner with his daughters Vivianne and Josephine Shane Tanner and his wife, Genevieve Ray Tanner, of Albuquerque, New Mexico: “I happen to fight, and the girls do too. So, we love UFC.”
One day prior to UFC Freedom 250, fans attended an event at The Ellipse that included a ceremonial weigh-in for the fighters. Temperatures in the afternoon reached 90 degrees Fahrenheit.
Portrait of two blonde haired, blue eyed women smiling.
Thousands of fans attended Saturday's event at The Ellipse, just outside the White House.
A split composite image of Jackie Chavez's face close up, left, and three men standing next to one another outside (from left, Henry Wied, George Wied, RJ Carrel)
Fans wore official UFC 250 merchandise at the festival, supporting their favorite fighters like Ilia Topuria and Justin Gaethje in the main event.
Fans watched as parachuters with flags and pyrotechnics jump over The Ellipse.
Fans wait for a turn to take photos inside an official UFC cage at the UFC 250 Freedom Fan Festival.

item.title: Trump, bald eagles and Zac Brown Band: Inside the UFC Freedom 250 Fan Fest

item.dc:creator: Peter Nicholas
Matt Dixon
Katherine Doyle
item.description: Facing one of the roughest patches of his second term, Trump is devoting outsize energy to giving Washington its biggest facelift in living memory.

media:content.media:text: President Donald Trump
media:content.media:description: President Donald Trump reacts during a Cabinet meeting at the White House on May 27, 2026.

media:content.media:text: The Washington Monument rises above the horizon and is reflected in the water of the reflecting pool.
media:content.media:description: The half-filled Reflecting Pool on the National Mall in Washington earlier this month.

item.title: Despite war, inflation and sinking polling, Trump takes comfort in giving D.C. a face-lift

item.dc:creator: Doha Madani
Tim Rohan
item.description: Four years after becoming the first Arab team to reach the World Cup semifinals, Morocco arrives with a roster of European stars — including Real Madrid’s Brahim Diaz and captain Achraf Hakimi, fresh of a Champions League title with Paris-St. Germain.

media:content.media:text: Brazil v Morocco world cup on-field action.
media:content.media:description: Ismael Saibari of Morocco celebrates scoring the opening goal during the FIFA World Cup 2026 Group C match against Morocco on Saturday.

item.title: Morocco’s draw against Brazil showed its potential to be the World Cup’s ultimate dark horse

item.dc:creator: The Associated Press
item.description: Kai Havertz scored two goals as Germany pulled away from World Cup first-timer Curaçao late in the opening half and piled on after that in a 7-1 win Sunday in a group match

media:content.media:text: Livano Comenencia slides on his knees on the ground of the soccer field in celebration as his teammates run behind him
media:content.media:description: Livano Comenencia of Curacao celebrates scoring his team's first goal during the FIFA World Cup 2026 Group E match against Germany on Sunday.

item.title: Curaçao celebrates historic first World Cup goal before Germany rolls to 7-1 victory

item.dc:creator: Andrew Greif
item.description: Pochettino, a 54-year-old Argentine, has been on the job for 20 months, but Friday’s game was unquestionably his biggest opportunity to make an impression on a wider fanbase tuning in to watch the U.S. for the first time.

media:content.media:text: USA v Paraguay: Group D - FIFA World Cup 2026
media:content.media:description: Mauricio Pochettino, head coach of the U.S. men, speaks to the media after the team's victory Friday.

item.title: Coaching the U.S. was a big test for Mauricio Pochettino. Then his World Cup opener went ‘amazing.’

item.dc:creator: Greg Rosenstein
item.description: Folarin Balogun, born in Brooklyn but raised in London, agreed to play his international career with the Americans in 2023. Three years later he opened the World Cup with two goals.

media:content.media:text: USA v Paraguay: Group D - FIFA World Cup 2026
media:content.media:description: Folarin Balogun #20 of the United States celebrates scoring his team's second goal during the FIFA World Cup 2026 Group D match between USA and Paraguay at Los Angeles Stadium on June 12, 2026 in Los Angeles, California.

item.title: Folarin Balogun chose the U.S. over England. It’s already paying off in the World Cup.

item.dc:creator: Angela Yang
item.description: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer is preparing to announce a package of measures Monday aimed at protecting children online, including possible restrictions on social media use for those under 16, according to a Downing Street spokesperson.“How we keep kids safe online is one of the biggest debates of our time.

media:content.media:text: Labour's Local Election Campaign Enters Final Days
media:content.media:description: U.K. Prime Minister Keir Starmer.

item.title: Keir Starmer set to unveil plans for new online protections for children

item.description: Japón aprovechó un tiro de esquina para fabricar una jugada de doble cabezazo dentro del área. Daichi Kamada apareció en el momento justo para rematar al fondo de la red y devolverle el empate a los Samurai Blue ante Países Bajos.

media:content.media:title: Gol de Daichi Kamada y vuelve el empate para Japón tras un doble cabezazo
media:content.media:description: Japón aprovechó un tiro de esquina para fabricar una jugada de doble cabezazo dentro del área. Daichi Kamada apareció en el momento justo para rematar al fondo de la red y devolverle el empate a los Samurai Blue ante Países Bajos.

item.title: Gol de Daichi Kamada y vuelve el empate para Japón tras un doble cabezazo

item.description: La Selección de Ecuador se hace presente en Pennsylvania, para afrontar a la competitiva Selección de Costa de Marfil.

media:content.media:title: Ecuador quiere demostrar que es el “Caballo Negro”, y llega a Philadelphia
media:content.media:description: La Selección de Ecuador se hace presente en Pennsylvania, para afrontar a la competitiva Selección de Costa de Marfil.

item.title: Ecuador quiere demostrar que es el “Caballo Negro”, y llega a Philadelphia

item.description: El zaguero alemán, que marcó un gol en su primer partido mundialista, señaló que deben trabajar en sus debilidades

media:content.media:title: Nathaniel Brown: “Es un sentimiento indescriptible”
media:content.media:description: El zaguero alemán, que marcó un gol en su primer partido mundialista, señaló que deben trabajar en sus debilidades

item.title: Nathaniel Brown: “Es un sentimiento indescriptible”

item.description: El jugador ecuatoriano destacó la preparación y motivación con la que su selección afronta el debut ante Costa de Marfil, y la importancia de llevarse los tres puntos para empezar a destacar en su grupo, frente a la clasificación.

media:content.media:title: Félix Torres: "Nos preparamos bien, trabajamos para el debut"
media:content.media:description: El jugador ecuatoriano destacó la preparación y motivación con la que su selección afronta el debut ante Costa de Marfil, y la importancia de llevarse los tres puntos para empezar a destacar en su grupo, frente a la clasificación.

item.title: Félix Torres: "Nos preparamos bien, trabajamos para el debut"

item.description: Ryan Gravenberch volvió a aparecer como asistente al habilitar a Crysencio Summerville, quien definió de zurda dentro del área para devolverle la ventaja a Países Bajos frente a Japón.

media:content.media:title: Gol de Crysencio Summerville devuelve la ventaja a Países Bajos ante Japón
media:content.media:description: Ryan Gravenberch volvió a aparecer como asistente al habilitar a Crysencio Summerville, quien definió de zurda dentro del área para devolverle la ventaja a Países Bajos frente a Japón.

item.title: Gol de Crysencio Summerville devuelve la ventaja a Países Bajos ante Japón

item.description: Tras una jugada a balón parado, Ryan Gravenberch envió un centro preciso al corazón del área, donde Virgil van Dijk se elevó por encima de todos para conectar un potente cabezazo y abrir el marcador para Países Bajos ante Japón.

media:content.media:title: ¡De cabeza! Virgil van Dijk se eleva por los aires para abrir el marcador de Países Bajos ante Japón
media:content.media:description: Tras una jugada a balón parado, Ryan Gravenberch envió un centro preciso al corazón del área, donde Virgil van Dijk se elevó por encima de todos para conectar un potente cabezazo y abrir el marcador para Países Bajos ante Japón.

item.title: ¡De cabeza! Virgil van Dijk se eleva por los aires para abrir el marcador de Países Bajos ante Japón

item.description: La fiel afición de la Selección de Japón y la “Naranja”, se apasionan con las acciones importantes en la Copa Mundial.

media:content.media:title: Así se vive el intenso sentimiento que genera Japón y Países Bajos en el Mundial
media:content.media:description: La fiel afición de la Selección de Japón y la “Naranja”, se apasionan con las acciones importantes en la Copa Mundial.

item.title: Así se vive el intenso sentimiento que genera Japón y Países Bajos en el Mundial

item.description: Manuel Neuer valoró el desempeño de Alemania en su estreno mundialista y destacó la concentración, el control del juego y la creatividad mostrada en ataque. El arquero también resaltó la importancia de mantener el nivel y seguir creciendo en el torneo.

media:content.media:title: Neuer destaca la mentalidad de Alemania tras un debut convincente
media:content.media:description: Manuel Neuer valoró el desempeño de Alemania en su estreno mundialista y destacó la concentración, el control del juego y la creatividad mostrada en ataque. El arquero también resaltó la importancia de mantener el nivel y seguir creciendo en el torneo.

item.title: Neuer destaca la mentalidad de Alemania tras un debut convincente

item.description: Aceptando que se enfrentaron con un equipo de calidad, el entrenador neerlandés de Curazao, Dick Advocaat, dice que concedieron los goles a Alemania con demasiada facilidad. Afirma que el juego sirve como lección para próximos enfrentamientos.

media:content.media:title: Dick Advocaat: “No debemos avergonzarnos”
media:content.media:description: Aceptando que se enfrentaron con un equipo de calidad, el entrenador neerlandés de Curazao, Dick Advocaat, dice que concedieron los goles a Alemania con demasiada facilidad. Afirma que el juego sirve como lección para próximos enfrentamientos.

item.title: Dick Advocaat: “No debemos avergonzarnos”

item.description: La Selección de Japón y los neerlandeses, están calentando los motores para un partido espectacular en la Copa Mundial de la FIFA 2026.

media:content.media:title: ¡No faltes! Japón y Países Bajos ya tienen todo listo para su arranque mundialista
media:content.media:description: La Selección de Japón y los neerlandeses, están calentando los motores para un partido espectacular en la Copa Mundial de la FIFA 2026.

item.title: ¡No faltes! Japón y Países Bajos ya tienen todo listo para su arranque mundialista

item.dc:creator: Kayla Hayempour
Mark Hodge
Amina Kilpatrick
item.description: An agreement between the United States and Iran to halt fighting and open the Strait of Hormuz has been reached, President Donald Trump announced Sunday.

media:content.media:text: The UFC arena on the South Lawn of the White House during a media preview on Thursday, June 11, 2026.
media:content.media:description: The UFC arena on the South Lawn of the White House during a media preview on Thursday, June 11, 2026.

media:content.media:text: Fans gathered Saturday afternoon on The Ellipse for Freedom 250’s “Fan Fest” event, a sprawling fair of booths and vendors set up in the park south of the White House.
media:content.media:description: Fans gathered Saturday afternoon on The Ellipse for Freedom 250’s “Fan Fest” event, a sprawling fair of booths and vendors set up in the park south of the White House.

item.title: Here’s the biggest news you missed this weekend

item.description: Butler county sheriff Brad Anderson answers questions about a plane crash near Kansas City, Missouri. According to Missouri State Highway Patrol, all 12 people on board the plane are presumed dead.

media:content.media:title: 12 presumed dead in plane crash near Kansas City, Missouri
media:content.media:description: Butler county sheriff Brad Anderson answers questions about a plane crash near Kansas City, Missouri. According to Missouri State Highway Patrol, all 12 people on board the plane are presumed dead.

item.title: 12 presumed dead in plane crash near Kansas City, Missouri

item.dc:creator: Alexandra Marquez
Frank Thorp V
item.description: Sen. Mitch McConnell, R-Ky., the former Senate majority leader, was hospitalized on Sunday, according to his spokesperson.“Senator McConnell was admitted to the hospital this morning,” spokesperson David Popp said in a statement.

media:content.media:text: Senate Army Budget Hearing
media:content.media:description: A spokesperson for Sen. Mitch McConnell, R-Ky., said he was admitted to the hospital on Sunday and is "receiving excellent care."

item.title: Sen. Mitch McConnell admitted to the hospital, spokesperson says

item.description: President Donald Trump called off strikes on Iran, saying a deal to end the war and reopen the Strait of Hormuz, a key trade route, would soon be “finalized.” NBC News' Richard Engel reports from Jerusalem on what could happen next and what Tehran is saying.

media:content.media:title: When could an Iran peace deal be finalized?
media:content.media:description: President Donald Trump called off strikes on Iran, saying a deal to end the war and reopen the Strait of Hormuz, a key trade route, would soon be “finalized.” NBC News' Richard Engel reports from Jerusalem on what could happen next and what Tehran is saying.

item.title: When could an Iran peace deal be finalized?

item.dc:creator: Angela Yang
item.description: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer is preparing to announce a package of measures Monday aimed at protecting children online, including possible restrictions on social media use for those under 16, according to a Downing Street spokesperson.“How we keep kids safe online is one of the biggest debates of our time.

media:content.media:text: Labour's Local Election Campaign Enters Final Days
media:content.media:description: U.K. Prime Minister Keir Starmer.

item.title: Keir Starmer set to unveil plans for new online protections for children

item.description: An agreement between the United States and Iran to halt fighting and open the Strait of Hormuz has been reached, President Donald Trump announced Sunday.

media:content.media:text: The UFC arena on the South Lawn of the White House during a media preview on Thursday, June 11, 2026.
media:content.media:description: The UFC arena on the South Lawn of the White House during a media preview on Thursday, June 11, 2026.

media:content.media:text: Fans gathered Saturday afternoon on The Ellipse for Freedom 250’s “Fan Fest” event, a sprawling fair of booths and vendors set up in the park south of the White House.
media:content.media:description: Fans gathered Saturday afternoon on The Ellipse for Freedom 250’s “Fan Fest” event, a sprawling fair of booths and vendors set up in the park south of the White House.

item.title: Here’s the biggest news you missed this weekend

item.dc:creator: Dennis Romero
Chantal Da Silva
item.description: An agreement has been reached between the United States and Iran, President Donald Trump and Pakistani Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif announced Sunday, with Iranian state media broadcasting the announcement.“The Deal with the Islamic Republic of Iran is now complete,” he said.

media:content.media:text: A rally in Tehran with a crowd of people waving flags.
media:content.media:description: People from Tehran province gather in support and allegiance to Iranian Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei, while holding Iranian flags and images of both the new and former leaders of Iran at Palestine Square in Tehran, on June 12, 2026.

item.title: Deal reached between the United States and Iran, Trump says
